a1ClaimsToRefine.explanation: Quote concatenates eight distinct Applicants-nav labels (ERAS, Stipends, Sample House Staff Contract, Training Programs, Benefits, Come to Nashville, Cost of Living, Further Graduate Study). Each is a separate Tier 2 signal and should be a discrete claim.

'Elective Away Rotations' is under House Staff nav and concerns VUMC residents going outbound, not USCE inbound visitors. Visibility CAUTION_SAFE_INTERNAL_REVIEW may overstate USCE relevance — FUTURE_LANE_ONLY is more appropriate.
'Visiting Residents' is a two-word nav label with no eligibility, fee, duration, or contact context. Flag for A3 to capture the sub-page before any downstream publication decision.
EOE boilerplate footer is generic institutional compliance text, not a Tier 3 careers/jobs signal. Tier 3 PHYSICIAN_CAREERS family assignment overstates the signal on a GME page.

additionalUnresolveds: Page mentions 'Visiting Residents' nav label but does not state eligibility, IMG acceptance, ECFMG/J-1 status, fees, or duration — sub-page capture required.
Page mentions 'Elective Away Rotations' under House Staff but does not state whether it covers only outbound VUMC residents or also inbound visiting trainees.
Page references 180 residency/fellowship programs but does not enumerate them or link a directory inline — 'Training Programs' sub-page capture required.
Page references ACGME website usage but does not list specific ACGME program IDs or accreditation statements.
Page has no observership/visiting medical student language — separate VUMC School of Medicine capture required to address pre-residency USCE.
recommendedA3Focus: Confirm no PUBLIC_SAFE_USCE claim is emitted from this GME_PAGE source.
Verify vumc-gme-006 Elective Away Rotations is treated as outbound trainee context, not inbound USCE.
Verify vumc-gme-007 Visiting Residents stays at Tier 2 trainee, not promoted to Tier 1 visiting medical student.
Confirm absence-of-observership on this page is NOT promoted to a negative refusal claim — no explicit refusal sentence present.
Verify vumc-gme-010 EOE footer is downgraded — boilerplate compliance text, not a careers signal.

a1ClaimsToRefine.explanation: Quote 'Physician\nCareers\nLearn More' is fragmented menu text. A stronger anchor (e.g. the 'Physician Careers' label on its own) would normalize better.

Nurse Residency Program is nursing post-licensure training, not physician GME. Lane RESIDENCY_PROGRAM_INFO risks downstream confusion with physician residency; consider PROVIDER_CAREERS / nursing-specific lane.
Single CONTACT_EMAIL claim bundles both email and phone; phone should be separated into its own CONTACT_PHONE claim (see a2_c4) for clean normalization.

additionalUnresolveds: Page references 'How to Apply' but the cleaned text does not contain the application instructions themselves.
Page references 'Our Benefits' link but the cleaned text does not enumerate specific benefits beyond 'start on Day One'.
Page references 'News and Events' for job opportunities but no specific event details are present in the cleaned text.
recommendedA3Focus: Confirm no PUBLIC_SAFE_USCE claim is emitted from this CAREERS_PORTAL source — all claims must remain FUTURE_LANE_ONLY.
Verify Nurse Residency Program claim (c3) is not classified as physician GME downstream.
Confirm CONTACT_EMAIL claim c4 is not double-counted against a2_c4 CONTACT_PHONE during dedupe.

newClaims.quote: You must check in with the GME office in person on day one of your rotation.
newClaims.claimText: Visiting residents must check in with the GME office in person on day one of their rotation.
newClaims.tierAssignmentRationale: Day-one in-person check-in requirement for visiting residents/fellows — Tier 2.
newClaims.whyA1Missed: Procedural in-person check-in requirement appears as a standalone bullet; A1 captured timeline/document items but skipped this onboarding step.

newClaims.quote: Your application must be submitted to the VUMC program at least 120 days beforehand and supporting documents (items 1-6, below) must be submitted to the VUMC program at least 90 days beforehand so that the rotation is fully onboarded 30 days from start date.
newClaims.claimText: Supporting documents (items 1-6) must be submitted ≥90 days before start date; rotation fully onboarded 30 days prior.
newClaims.tierAssignmentRationale: Supporting-document 90-day deadline is distinct from A1's 120-day application deadline — Tier 2.
newClaims.whyA1Missed: A1 captured the 120-day application and 100-day PLA deadlines but missed the separate 90-day supporting-documents deadline.

newClaims.quote: If applicable, a copy of a valid ECFMG certificate and Visa.You must complete the following at least 60 days in advance:
newClaims.claimText: Immunization Form and Cisive background check must be completed ≥60 days prior to start date.
newClaims.tierAssignmentRationale: 60-day pre-arrival deadline for immunization and background check — Tier 2.
newClaims.whyA1Missed: A1 captured immunization and background-check requirements but missed the explicit 60-day deadline associated with both.

newClaims.quote: Office of Inspector General/Excluded Individuals - In order to comply with federal law, VUMC will check all visiting house staff against the Office of Inspector General's list of individuals excluded from federal healthcare programs. Any visiting house staff identified as excluded must be terminated from the visiting residency training program and home institution will be notified.
newClaims.claimText: VUMC checks all visiting house staff against OIG excluded-individuals list; excluded individuals are terminated.
newClaims.tierAssignmentRationale: OIG exclusion screening for visiting residents — Tier 2 trainee screening requirement.
newClaims.whyA1Missed: A1 captured the Cisive background check but missed the separate OIG database screening requirement.

newClaims.quote: National Practitioners Data Bank (NPDB) - All visiting house staff will be checked against the NPDB.
newClaims.claimText: All visiting house staff are checked against the National Practitioners Data Bank (NPDB).
newClaims.tierAssignmentRationale: NPDB screening for visiting residents — Tier 2.
newClaims.whyA1Missed: A1 covered Cisive background but missed the additional NPDB database check listed under screening.

additionalUnresolveds: Page is exclusively for visiting residents/fellows (house staff); does not explicitly state whether pre-residency visiting medical students are accepted or directed elsewhere — no refusal sentence either way.
recommendedA3Focus: Confirm no PUBLIC_SAFE_USCE claim is emitted from this URL — the page is Tier 2 visiting residents/fellows only.
Verify A1's contact claims (c_vumc_visres_013, c_vumc_visres_014) are scoped to GME visiting-residents pathway, not generic USCE.
